topic.what: Contextual integrity privacy risks in enterprise LLM agents using dense retrieval
topic.how: CI-Work benchmark with 125 seeds across 5 info-flow directions evaluating leakage, violation, and conveyance rates
topic.why_matters: Reveals persistent privacy-utility trade-off and inverse scaling where larger models leak more sensitive info

characteristics.what_it_measures: Proportion of test cases where any sensitive entry is disclosed
Proportion of sensitive entries disclosed in agent responses
Proportion of essential entries successfully retrieved and transmitted
characteristics.context: Trade-off: higher conveyance correlates with more leakage (r=0.40)

characteristics.what_it_measures: How entry count affects privacy violations
characteristics.evidence: Figure 4a showing violation rises as context entries increase from 1 to 12
characteristics.context: Multi-entity contexts introduce interference impairing CI adherence

characteristics.what_it_measures: How entry length affects privacy violations
characteristics.evidence: Figure 4b showing violation increases from short to very-long entries
characteristics.context: Richer detail enables better task grounding but expands disclosure surface

characteristics.what_it_measures: Inverse scaling phenomenon where larger models leak more
characteristics.evidence: Figure 6a: GPT-4.1-nano < GPT-4.1-mini < GPT-4.1 for leakage
characteristics.context: Attributed to stronger sycophantic behavior prioritizing user instructions

characteristics.what_it_measures: Effect of chain-of-thought reasoning on privacy
characteristics.evidence: Figure 6b showing minimal leakage reduction with high reasoning
characteristics.context: CoT optimizes for task fulfillment not privacy norms

characteristics.what_it_measures: How user instructions affect privacy leakage
characteristics.value: Explicit pressure nearly doubles violation rate
characteristics.evidence: Figure 5: +31% violation increase under explicit user pressure
characteristics.context: Dual collapse: agents leak more while conveying less essential content

characteristics.evidence: Table 2 showing CI-CoT preserves more utility than generic Prompt Defense
characteristics.context: CI-CoT achieves 9% privacy gain while only losing 9% conveyance vs 12% loss for generic defense

novelty: First enterprise-specific CI benchmark with parallel info-flows
Quantifies privacy-utility trade-off with empirical evidence
Documents inverse scaling phenomenon in privacy leakage
Demonstrates user pressure amplifies both leakage and violations
open_problems: Context-aware privacy mechanisms not yet developed
Training-time alignment for enterprise privacy needed
Scalable defenses for large-scale enterprise data required
